sources.title: Moana (restored, colorized) (1926, documentary, imdb score: 6.8)
sources.description: Moana is a 1926 American silent documentary film , or more strictly a work of docufiction , which was directed by Robert J. Flaherty , creator of Nanook of the North (1922).

metadata.Plot: Moana Waialiki is a sea voyaging enthusiast and the only daughter of a chief in a long line of navigators. When her island's fishermen can't catch any fish and the crops fail, she learns that the demigod Maui caused the blight by stealing the heart of the goddess, Te Fiti. The only way to heal the island is to persuade Maui to return Te Fiti's heart, so Moana sets off on an epic journey across the Pacific. The film is based on stories from Polynesian mythology.
metadata.Director: Ron Clements, John Musker, Don Hall
metadata.Writer: Jared Bush, Ron Clements, John Musker
metadata.Actors: Auli'i Cravalho, Dwayne Johnson, Rachel House
metadata.Awards: Nominated for 2 Oscars. 22 wins & 90 nominations total
